title: 2026 Golden Globes Recap: Key Winners and Moments
text: The 83rd Golden Globe Awards, held January 11, 2026, celebrated achievements across film, television, and podcasts for the first time. Host Nikki Glaser delivered sharp commentary throughout the ceremony in Beverly Hills. "One Battle After Another," Paul Thomas Anderson's comedy-drama, emerged as the evening's biggest film winner, claiming four awards including Best Picture — Musical or Comedy and Best Director. Teyana Taylor received Best Supporting Actress, delivering what the article describes as "a highlight" acceptance speech about representation. The historical drama "Hamnet" secured Best Picture — Drama, with Jessie Buckley winning Best Actress. Timothée Chalamet captured his inaugural Golden Globe for Best Actor in a Musical or Comedy for his role in "Marty Supreme." Netflix's limited series "Adolescence" dominated with four wins across all nominated categories. Noah Wyle won Best Actor in a Drama Series for "The Pitt," while Seth Rogen took Best Actor in a Comedy Series for Apple TV's "The Studio." The ceremony introduced podcasts as an award category. Amy Poehler's "Good Hang with Amy Poehler" won the inaugural award, marking the Globes' recognition of "how much audio storytelling has grown." The animated film "KPop Demon Hunters" won Best Original Song, with its young co-writer delivering an emotional acceptance speech about perseverance.
